questions.question: Which statement best describes UV-Vis spectroscopy?
questions.option_a: Measures optical rotation for chiral compounds
questions.option_d: Quantifies absorbance due to electronic transitions; follows Beer–Lambert law
questions.explanation: Concentration is proportional to absorbance (A=εbc) under linear conditions.

questions.question: Which statement best describes IR spectroscopy?
questions.option_a: Determines elemental composition via atomic absorption
questions.option_b: Identifies functional groups via vibrational transitions
questions.option_d: Quantifies absorbance due to electronic transitions; follows Beer–Lambert law
questions.explanation: Characteristic peaks correspond to bond vibrations.

questions.question: Which statement best describes NMR spectroscopy?
questions.option_a: Provides structural information via nuclear spin transitions
questions.option_b: Closeness of measured value to true value
questions.option_c: Separates analytes based on interactions with stationary/mobile phases
questions.option_d: Separates volatile analytes in a gas phase
questions.explanation: Chemical shift reflects electronic environment; splitting indicates coupling.

questions.question: Which statement best describes HPLC?
questions.option_a: Separates analytes based on interactions with stationary/mobile phases
questions.option_c: Measures optical rotation for chiral compounds
questions.option_d: Provides structural information via nuclear spin transitions
questions.explanation: Reverse-phase uses nonpolar stationary phase; more hydrophobic retains longer.

questions.question: Which statement best describes GC?
questions.option_a: Identifies functional groups via vibrational transitions
questions.option_d: Separates volatile analytes in a gas phase
questions.explanation: Requires volatility/thermal stability; detector choices include FID/MS.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Titration (acid-base)?
questions.option_a: Closeness of measured value to true value
questions.option_c: Separates based only on size exclusion
questions.explanation: Indicators/pH meters used; equivalence point depends on acid/base strength.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Validation: accuracy?
questions.option_a: Closeness of measured value to true value
questions.option_c: Separates based only on size exclusion
questions.option_d: Identifies functional groups via vibrational transitions
questions.explanation: Assessed with recovery studies or reference standards.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Validation: precision?
questions.option_c: Closeness of measured value to true value
questions.option_d: Identifies functional groups via vibrational transitions
questions.explanation: Expressed as %RSD; includes intra-/inter-day precision.

questions.question: Which statement best describes LOD/LOQ?
questions.option_d: Separates volatile analytes in a gas phase
questions.explanation: Based on signal-to-noise or SD/slope approaches.

questions.question: Which statement best describes System suitability?
questions.option_a: Measures optical rotation for chiral compounds
questions.explanation: Parameters include plate count, tailing, resolution, %RSD.

questions.question: Resolution (Rs): Which option correctly completes 'Improving Rs is most directly achieved by ...'?
questions.option_c: increasing selectivity (α) or efficiency (N) or adjusting retention (k')
questions.explanation: Resolution improves with better selectivity/efficiency and appropriate retention.

questions.question: Tailing factor: Which option correctly completes 'A tailing factor >1 often suggests ...'?
questions.explanation: Peak tailing indicates asymmetry due to interactions/overload.

questions.question: Internal standard method: Which option correctly completes 'Best used to ...'?
questions.explanation: Internal standards improve quantitative robustness.

questions.question: Calibration curve: Which option correctly completes 'Linearity refers to ...'?
questions.explanation: Linearity ensures accurate quantitation across the range.
